Nathan Kling

Turning AI ambition into organizational reality.
Strategy. Transformation. Delivery.

Let's Talk

About

Most organizations aren't failing at AI because of the technology. They're failing at the organizational muscle to absorb it. I build that muscle.

Over fifteen years leading professional services teams, delivering complex programs, and driving organizational change — the kind that survives first contact with reality. I've built and scaled delivery organizations, led enterprise transformations, and now focus on the gap most companies can't close: turning AI strategy into operational capability.

The hard part isn't the technology. It's the leadership alignment, the capability building, the change management that separates a pilot from a practice. That's where I work.

MBA and Master of Music — analytical rigor meets creative problem-solving. I think in systems, communicate in stories, and ship on deadlines.

Open to

  • Executive leadership roles
  • Fractional / interim engagements
  • Advisory & board positions

Writing

Thoughts on AI adoption, leadership, and the human side of transformation.

LinkedIn March 6, 2026

Friday Reflection: The EQ Gap

Why your AI adoption strategy is failing at the human layer — and what to do about it.

Medium March 6, 2026

The EQ Gap: Why Your AI Adoption Strategy Is Failing at the Human Layer

LinkedIn March 5, 2026

What AI Can't Fix (Because You Broke It First)

Trust deficits predate AI — AI just reveals them. You can't borrow against trust you haven't built.

Medium March 4, 2026

AI That Sticks

LinkedIn February 27, 2026

AI Doesn't Create Your Culture. It Reveals It.

AI is a Rorschach test for organizational culture. Governance reflects trust — it doesn't create it.

Medium February 24, 2026

The Doom Loop Gets the Headlines. Here’s What I’m Actually Seeing.
